rationale: Both papers study antecedents of positive WOM rather than outcomes: the citing paper models product-level drivers (including public visibility) of immediate vs ongoing WOM, while the cited paper theorizes and tests how need for uniqueness and public vs private consumption affect WOM generation. The overlap in focal constructs (WOM drivers, visibility/publicness) and even shared campaign context (BzzAgent appears in both notes) makes this more than an obviously incidental citation. With only DOI-level match evidence but strong topical/theoretical alignment, this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
